job_statement: When I have deployed Argo CD applications, I want to monitor their health status, so that I can detect and respond to synchronization issues before they impact users
context: After installing GitOps Operator and deploying Argo CD applications in a namespace
functional_need: Monitor the health status of Argo CD applications
emotional_need: Feel confident that application issues will be detected early
social_need: Maintain reliability expectations for development teams
success_criteria: Health metrics are automatically collected without manual configuration
Can view health status across all applications in a namespace
Receive alerts when applications are out of sync
evidence: monitoring-argo-cd-instances.adoc -> Introduction section, lines 9-9 [assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-instances.adoc]; modules/gitops-monitoring-argo-cd-health-using-prometheus-metrics.adoc -> Section 'Monitoring Argo CD health using Prometheus metrics', lines 1-24 [module: modules/gitops-monitoring-argo-cd-health-using-prometheus-metrics.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: The GitOps Operator automatically connects Argo CD instances to the cluster monitoring stack. Source module: modules/gitops-monitoring-argo-cd-health-using-prometheus-metrics.adoc (PROCEDURE). Assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-instances.adoc

job_statement: When I need to investigate Argo CD application issues, I want to query Prometheus metrics for health status, so that I can understand which applications are unhealthy and why
context: Troubleshooting Argo CD application synchronization or health issues
functional_need: Query and visualize Argo CD health metrics using Prometheus
emotional_need: Feel empowered with diagnostic tools to investigate issues
social_need: Demonstrate technical competence in resolving platform issues
success_criteria: Can access Prometheus metrics through OpenShift web console
Can filter metrics by namespace and health status
Metrics provide actionable information about application state
evidence: modules/gitops-monitoring-argo-cd-health-using-prometheus-metrics.adoc -> Section 'Monitoring Argo CD health using Prometheus metrics', lines 9-23 [module: modules/gitops-monitoring-argo-cd-health-using-prometheus-metrics.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Users navigate to Developer perspective -> Observe -> Metrics and run custom PromQL queries. Example query: sum(argocd_app_info{dest_namespace=~"<namespace>",health_status!=""}) by (health_status). Source module: modules/gitops-monitoring-argo-cd-health-using-prometheus-metrics.adoc (PROCEDURE). Assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-instances.adoc

job_statement: When I install GitOps Operator, I want monitoring to be automatically configured, so that I don't have to manually set up observability infrastructure
context: Initial setup of GitOps in an OpenShift cluster
functional_need: Automatic integration between Argo CD and cluster monitoring stack
emotional_need: Feel relieved that monitoring works out-of-the-box
social_need: Meet organizational requirements for observability without extra effort
success_criteria: Monitoring is enabled by default when GitOps Operator detects Argo CD
No manual configuration required to collect basic metrics
Alerts are automatically configured for out-of-sync applications
notes: The GitOps Operator automatically detects Argo CD instances in the defined namespace (e.g., openshift-gitops) and connects them to the monitoring stack, providing alerts for out-of-sync applications. Source assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-instances.adoc
